AN OFFICIAL WITH THE BROWNSVILLE-SOUTH PADRE ISLAND INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT REPORTED SOME MINOR DAMAGE TO A FEW HANGERS, INCLUDING A PORTION OF THE ROOF OF A HANGER CAVED IN SLIGHTLY, ALLOWING WATER TO ENTER THE HANGER. THE GARAGE DOOR ON ANOTHER HANGER ON THE WEST SIDE OF THE AIRPORT CAVED IN. THEY ALSO REPORTED SOME MINOR FLOODING OF THE TERMINAL DURING THE HEIGHT OF THE HEAVY RAIN LAST NIGHT. Public reported estimated ping pong size hail on FM 3167 in Rio Grande City with picture on social media. Time estimated from radar.
The Texas Coastal Ocean Observing Network site at Rincon del San Jose gusted to 44 knots at 4:54PM CDT.
Valley International Airport gusted to 82 mph at 731 PM CDT.
An NWS Employee sent in a picture of a power pole leaning on Grimes Rd east of the Fire Station. Time estimated by radar and the wind gust recorded at KHRL during the event.
A member of the public reported quarter sized hail at 8:07 PM in north Brownsville through social media.
Member of the public shared a picture of hail with an estimated size of 2 inches from the corner of FM1421 and FM1732 in Cameron County. Time estimated by radar.
A member of the public recorded a video showing quarter sized hail and gusty winds at a self car wash at the intersection of Canton and Raul Longoria.
Member of the public shared pictures of three hail stones all larger than a quarter. They were estimated to be about the size of a golf ball. Time estimated by radar.
Trained spotter called to report half dollar sized hail at Riverbend Resort in Brownsville, TX.
A member of the public reported golf ball sized hail in west Brownsville at 8:15 PM through social media.
The Texas Coastal Ocean Observing Network site at Rincon del San Jose recorded a wind gust of 59 knots, or 68 mph, at 1:48PM CDT.
Wind gusts associated a thunderstorm reported by buoy station 42020 reported 37 knot winds.
Member of the public shared a picture of a hail stone estimated to be around 2 inches. Timing based on radar.
Member of the public shared a picture of quarter size hail in the San Benito area. Timing based report given by the public and radar.
Canopy lifted from tented play area at the Centro Cultural of Cameron Park, per photos from EMC. Wind speed unknown.
Member of the public shared a picture of a large palm tree down across the westbound lane of Coffee Road, just west of Casa de Oro. Timing based on radar.
Member of the public shared a picture of a telephone pole snapped in half near Perez Elementary School at the corner of Renfro Blvd and Shidler Drive in Brownsville. Timing based on radar.
Public relayed via social media that RV jack was bent, and trailer pushed back about 2 feet due to thunderstorm winds. Time approximated by radar.
NWS employee reported sign damage at Dunkin Donuts and Popeyes on Boca Chica Blvd. Time estimated by radar.
PEAK WIND GUSTS OF 44 KNOTS AT 712 WITH GUSTS 34 KNOTS OR GREATER THROUGH 742 AM.
PEAK WIND GUSTS OF 44 KNOTS AT 712 WITH GUSTS 34 KNOTS OR GREATER THROUGH 742 AM.
RECORDED AT PORT ISABEL NOAA/CO-OPS SITE.
BRAZOS SANTIAGO MARINE PLATFORM ALONG SOUTH PADRE ISLAND RECORDED 44 KNOT GUST AT 724 AM. DURATION OF 34 KNOT OR HIGHER GUSTS CONTINUED THROUGH 736 AM.
AT THE SOUTH PADRE ISLAND COAST GUARD STATION. DURATION OF WINDS 39 MPH...34 KNOTS...OR HIGHER WAS FROM 724 THROUGH 736 AM.

An NWS Employee witnessed a landspout along FM802 (Ruben Torres Blvd) just west of the railroad tracks between Central and Minnesota on their commute to work. No damage was observed at the time of the report.
Pictures from social media shows a metal structure crumpled up and blown several yards into the field across the street. Timing estimated by radar.
Pictures from social media show awning/roof damage to The End of the Trail Sports Bar #1. Timing estimate by radar.

A member of the public shared a video of a landspout near the levee just east of La Villa. The video shows a condensation funnel reaching about halfway up to the base of cumulus clouds that were part of an outflow boundary in eastern Hildago County. No damage or injuries were reported at the time of this report.
The ASOS at Harlingen Airport gusted to 71 mph at 4:08PM CDT.
The ASOS station at the Valley International Airport in Harlingen gusted to 74 mph at 4:19PM CDT.

Gale force gusts began at 1206 PM and continued to 1230 PM. Location in Laguna Madre just south of Baffin Bay, which was where the observing platform is (immediately north of this location, proxy location).
The NOAA Buoy 42020 recorded a peak wind gust of 41 knots as the leading edge of a squall line passed through at 1 PM. The gusts continued to between 120 and 130 PM.

The Texas Coastal Ocean Observing Network (TCOON) observing platform at Rincon del San Jose (Laguna Madre in Kenedy County) recorded a peak gust of 42.9 knots at 118 PM. Initial gusts reached 38.1 knots at 1 pm and continued at gale force through 242 pm.
The TCOON platorm at Realitos Peninsula recorded a peak wind gust of 40 knots between 254 and 3 PM CDT. Start time of gale force winds (35 knots) began at 248 PM.
The TCOON platform at Brazos Santiago recorded a peak gust of 45 knots at 4 PM, with gusts of gale force (41 knots) beginning at 324 PM and continuing through at least 424 PM.

The C-MAN platform at the South Padre Island Coast Guard station recorded a peak gusts of 34 knots at 336 PM, associated with the passage of a squall line. Gusts remained near 30 knots for another half hour as a mesohigh maintained strong northerly flow.
